message:
test(engine): add attestation handshake tamper-detection integration tests and reformat assert macros

Three new integration tests that verify the attestation handshake
rejects tampered inputs at each stage of the verification pipeline:

- handshake_rejects_tampered_challenge_signature: mutates
  policy_plane_signature on a valid challenge to [0xCA, 0xFE] and
  verifies verify_and_authorize() returns ChallengeSignatureInvalid.
  Ensures the new challenge-signature verification step (added in
  attestation_handshake.rs) is exercised end-to-end.

- handshake_rejects_tampered_quote_after_response_signing: modifies
  attestation_quote.issued_at_ns after the client signs its response,
  causing the response signature to no longer match the canonical bytes
  of the quote. Verifies ResponseSignatureInvalid error.

- authorization_tampering_is_rejected: completes a full handshake,
  then serializes the verifier to JSON, injects an unauthorized
  "admin_override" operation into the authorization, deserializes
  back, and checks that check_authorization() returns
  AuthorizationSignatureInvalid. Validates that authorization structs
  are integrity-sealed against post-hoc modification.

Also applies rustfmt to two existing assert!() macros in
revoke_one_cell_leaves_others test (condensed multi-line style).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
